summary: A brief LinkedIn profile snippet for Graham Colditz, highlighting his roles at Washington University School of Medicine, his focus on breast cancer prevention, and his advocacy for transdisciplinary training in public health.

principles.statement: Addressing complex cancer prevention questions requires transdisciplinary collaboration.
principles.rationale: Cancer prevention involves multilevel and multifaceted issues that cannot be fully understood or solved from the perspective of a single discipline.
principles.source_quote: ...addressing the complex questions around cancer prevention and control increasingly requires the ability to work and communicate across disciplines in order to gain a perspective that encompasses the multilevel and multifaceted issues involved with this public health issue.

signature_quotes.text: ...addressing the complex questions around cancer prevention and control increasingly requires the ability to work and communicate across disciplines in order to gain a perspective that encompasses the multilevel and multifaceted issues involved with this public health issue.
signature_quotes.context: From a publication abstract on training transdisciplinary scholars in cancer prevention and control.

anti_patterns.description: Focusing postdoctoral training programs solely within a single discipline or closely related fields.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to equip scholars with the broad perspective needed to address the multilevel and multifaceted issues inherent in complex public health problems like cancer prevention.
